name: pyimgui (Python Bindings for Dear ImGui)

agent_readiness.notes: Python bindings for Dear ImGui — immediate mode GUI. import imgui. from imgui.integrations.glfw import GlfwRenderer. imgui.create_context(). window = glfw.create_window(1280, 720, 'App', None, None). impl = GlfwRenderer(window). while not glfw.window_should_close(window): imgui.new_frame(). if imgui.begin('Panel'): imgui.text('Hello'). changed, value = imgui.input_float('Value', 1.0). imgui.end(). imgui.render(). impl.render(imgui.get_draw_data()). glfw.poll_events(). Integrations: GLFW, SDL2, pygame, pyglet. Used for game dev debug UIs, scientific visualization overlays, tool development.

gotchas: Steep learning curve — immediate mode paradigm different from retained GUI toolkits
pyimgui development slower than imgui-bundle (newer alternative)
No native look and feel — custom rendering only
Documentation is thin — often requires reading Dear ImGui C++ docs
best_when: Debug/tool overlays in OpenGL games or simulations
Quick parameter tweaking UIs in Python OpenGL apps
When Dear ImGui's immediate mode is required
avoid_when: Users expecting native OS look and feel
When imgui-bundle provides better maintained alternative
Teams unfamiliar with OpenGL rendering pipelines
summary: pyimgui provides Python bindings for Dear ImGui — the popular immediate mode GUI for game tools and debug UIs. imgui.new_frame(); imgui.begin('Panel'); imgui.text('Hello'); imgui.end(). GLFW/SDL2/pygame integrations. Immediate mode: UI declared per frame, not retained. BSD-2. Used for debug overlays in OpenGL apps. Consider imgui-bundle or DearPyGui as better-maintained alternatives for new projects.
